prompt: Add a fictional column labeled "Podcasts" with semi-random data aligning with the existing format.
prompt_refined: Edit image 1. Insert a new column labeled 'Podcasts' into the main table. This column should be placed immediately to the right of the 'Social Media' column. Populate the 'Podcasts' column with semi-random data for each corresponding row (e.g., Revenue, Impressions, Clicks, Readers, Likes, Conversions, Segments Reached). Ensure the data types (e.g., currency, percentage, numerical values, and comma-separated text lists) and visual formatting (e.g., font style, size, color scheme, cell backgrounds, and alignment) are consistent with the existing table structure and data in image 1.
objects: Main table with 'Content Marketing' column and its row labels
'Podcasts' column header, located immediately to the right of 'Social Media'
Semi-random data values populating the 'Podcasts' column
Four horizontal bar charts for Blog, Video, Forums, and Social Media
